queries: This section identifies the core project metadata, including the project name, project number, and physical location.
This text defines the primary parties involved in the agreement, specifically naming the owner, general contractor, construction manager, and the subcontractor.
This page contains the formal agreement date, the prime contract date, and execution signatures.
This section establishes the financial terms, defining the total contract sum, the lump sum subcontract price, and the schedule of values.
This defines the percentage of retainage held from progress payments, such as a ten percent retainage or ninety percent payment terms.
This text outlines the post-completion warranty period, specifically noting the standard one year warranty and correction period obligations.
This section details the specific scope of work, outlining exactly what materials and labor the subcontractor shall furnish and install.
This text explicitly lists the inclusions and exclusions to the scope, clarifying what is not included or what is contractor or owner furnished.
This references the plans, specifications, and exhibit scope documents that dictate the technical requirements of the work.
This section establishes the project schedule, including the estimated start date, milestones, sequencing, and date of substantial completion.
This text covers delay terms, including time is of the essence clauses, procedures for extension of time, and acceleration of work.
This defines the financial penalties for schedule failures, including no damages for delay limitations and liquidated damages provisions.
This section outlines payment contingencies, including pay-if-paid, pay-when-paid, and condition precedent to payment clauses where the subcontractor assumes the risk of owner nonpayment.
This details the invoicing process, including the application for payment, billing deadlines, and the requirement to be paid within a specific number of days after the contractor receives payment.

This covers financial protections and deductions, including the submission of conditional and unconditional lien waivers, the contractor's right to withholding payment, offset, backcharges, and payment for off-site stored materials.

This is the indemnification clause requiring the subcontractor to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the contractor and owner from claims, including broad form indemnity and action over employee injury claims.
This outlines liability limitations regarding sole negligence, partial negligence, and compliance with anti-indemnity acts such as Texas Insurance Code Chapter 151.

This specifies the insurance requirements and policy limits, including commercial general liability, automobile liability, umbrella and excess liability, workers compensation, employers liability, and the requirement to name additional insureds.
This section states which documents are incorporated by reference into the agreement, including the prime contract, general conditions, and specific contract documents.
This contains the flow-down provisions stating that the subcontractor assumes all obligations and responsibilities toward the contractor that the contractor assumes toward the owner.
This text lists the attached exhibits and attachments that form the complete subcontract agreement.
This section explains the contractor's rights regarding termination for cause and termination for convenience.
This details the procedures for subcontractor default, including the required notice of default and the applicable cure period.
This outlines the required dispute resolution processes, including mediation, binding arbitration, litigation, the designated venue, and the governing law.
This section covers federal and state labor compliance requirements, including Davis-Bacon act provisions, certified payroll submission, and prevailing wage mandates for HUD projects.
This outlines specific operational requirements including jobsite safety requirements, OSHA compliance, and mandatory reporting requirements.
